CALL_911: Breathing stops or becomes extremely difficult
Severe bleeding that cannot be controlled
Symptoms rapidly worsen in any way
GO_TO_ER: Difficulty breathing or shortness of breath
Severe bleeding that will not stop
Stroke symptoms: face drooping, arm weakness, speech difficulty
You feel unable to safely drive to the hospital
URGENT_CARE: Vomiting blood or blood in stool
Chest pain or difficulty breathing develops
Severe headache or worst headache of your life
You feel something is seriously wrong
MONITOR: Chest pain or difficulty breathing develops
Vomiting blood or blood in stool
You feel instinctively that something is wrong

mild_headache: Headache becomes the worst of your life
Headache with stiff neck or fever
high_fever: Fever rises above 103°F in adults or 104°F in children
Fever with severe headache and stiff neck
Fever with rash that does not blanch when pressed
Fever with confusion or altered behavior
Fever persists longer than 3 days without improvement
high_fever_infant: Fever above 100.4°F in infant under 3 months — go immediately
Infant becomes difficult to wake or unusually limp
Fever with a bulging soft spot on the head
Fever with a rash that does not blanch
Infant refuses all feeds for more than 8 hours
chest_pain: Pain spreads to arm, jaw, neck, or back
Pain with sweating, nausea, or lightheadedness
difficulty_breathing: Breathing becomes extremely labored or stops
Lips, fingernails, or skin turn blue
Chest pain develops alongside breathing difficulty
wheezing: Wheezing becomes severe or breathing is labored
Lips or skin begin to turn blue
Child cannot speak or cry normally
Inhaler provides no relief after two doses
nausea: Unable to keep down any fluids for 8 or more hours
Signs of dehydration: no urination, extreme thirst, severe dizziness
Vomiting with severe headache or stiff neck
vomiting_infant: Infant cannot keep down any feeds for more than 6 hours
Signs of dehydration: sunken fontanelle, no wet diapers, dry mouth
Vomit contains blood or green bile
Infant becomes unusually drowsy or limp
Severe abdominal pain or bloating develops
dizziness: Dizziness with sudden numbness or weakness
Dizziness with vision changes or double vision
severe_bleeding: Bleeding cannot be controlled with firm pressure after 10 minutes
Blood is spurting or pulsing from the wound
Person shows signs of shock: pale, cold, clammy skin
Bleeding from a major body area such as the neck or abdomen
confusion: Confusion becomes severe or person cannot recognize familiar people
Person loses consciousness or cannot be woken
Weakness or numbness on one side of the body
sudden_confusion_elderly: Confusion becomes severe or worsens rapidly
Person cannot state their name or location
New weakness or inability to stand develops
Person loses consciousness or cannot be roused
fall_with_injury: Head injury with loss of consciousness, even briefly
Inability to bear weight or move the injured area
Visible bone deformity or bone breaking the skin
Numbness or tingling in the affected limb
fall_unable_to_get_up: Person cannot move one or both legs
Severe hip or back pain preventing any movement
Person has been on the floor for more than 1 hour
Signs of shock: pale, cold, or clammy skin
Head injury occurred during the fall
seizure: Person does not regain consciousness after seizure stops
Person is injured during the seizure
stroke_symptoms: Any new face drooping, arm weakness, or speech difficulty — call 911 immediately
Sudden vision loss in one or both eyes
Sudden severe headache with no known cause
Sudden loss of balance or coordination
rapid_heartrate: Heart rate remains above 150 beats per minute
Racing heart with chest pain or shortness of breath
Heart rate is irregular and very fast
low_oxygen: Lips or skin begin to turn blue
Cannot speak in full sentences due to breathlessness
Confusion or altered mental state develops
rash_with_fever_child: Rash does not blanch or fade when pressed with a glass
Rash spreads rapidly within minutes to hours
Child becomes very drowsy, limp, or difficult to wake
Stiff neck develops alongside rash and fever
Rash appears purplish or blood-red in color
urinary_symptoms_elderly: Flank or back pain develops — possible kidney involvement
Unable to urinate despite feeling the urge
Blood in urine that is significant or persistent
